title: Scorzoneroides palisiae
taxonomy: Scorzoneroides palisiae is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is further classified under the order Asterales. It belongs to the tribe Cardueae, which includes thistles and related plants. Within the genus Scorzoneroides, S. palisiae is one of the species known for its unique characteristics. The classification of S. palisiae is based on its morphological and genetic features.

intro: Sorzoneroides palisiae, also known as Palisia's scorzoneroid, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, Palisia's scorzoneroid has been used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Palisia's scorzoneroid include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Palisia's scorzoneroid is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential to soothe digestive issues and boost immune function.
